subject: [SO] SO Istanbul first release candidate
commit_message:
[SO] SO Istanbul first release candidate

Includes the So core components.  Also installs compatible
CDS version 1.1.5

Issue-ID: SO-3740

Signed-off-by: seshukm <seshu.kumar.m@huawei.com>
Change-Id: Ic40426e569fd9f15e50d6697eb031f169c7eb38b
Signed-off-by: Dan Timoney <dtimoney@att.com>

and those haven't changed in a while so not sure why it should have stopped working all of a sudden.  I noticed in yesterday's daily gating that one of the policy healtchecks timed out, but it was working earlier this week.  Not sure if that's what happened here - where can we find the results?

ok
so it is probably not reproducible as such
the basic_clamp which interacts with policy is fine

the log of the healthcheck are there

Patch Set 2:

thanks for the links.  It appears that policy/drools (pdpd) didn't come up in time.  Looking back over past gating results, I see occasional failure because one of the PDPs didn't come up (apex-pdp, drools-pdp).  So maybe this is just one of those intermittent issues.

The failure in yesterday's gating test was due to a timeout, which is a completely different issue.

Patch Set 2:

If my recollection is correct that there has not been changes from policy side since the beginning of istanbul release in master, I would tend to think that these may be either sporadic errors or some changes underneath.    Looking some into the logs I see issues connecting to dmaap by drools-pdp, but in contrast other policy components have no issues for the same topic, all of the policy components use the same libraries so that's strange that some are successful getting to the message-router and others don't.   For example:

drools (rolling errors talking to dmaap):
[2021-08-19T18:52:39.082+00:00|INFO|HttpClient|DMAAP-source-POLICY-PDP-PAP] GET https://message-router:3905/events/POLICY-PDP-PAP/3991b3eb-e81b-4f46-9b3e-2edd72c2aa91/onap-policy-drools-pdp-0?timeout=15000&limit=100 (anonymous) ...
[2021-08-19T18:52:40.117+00:00|WARN|HttpClient|DMAAP-source-POLICY-PDP-PAP] Error executing HTTP request. Connect to message-router:3905 [message-router/10.0.233.91] failed: Connection refused (Connection refused); blacklisting for 2 minutes

xacml (all OK for same url and topic using the same libraries):
[2021-08-19T19:52:42.316+00:00|INFO|HttpClient|DMAAP-source-POLICY-PDP-PAP] GET https://message-router:3905/events/POLICY-PDP-PAP/9d1ddf70-12be-41c9-a812-a3fb433faa1b/onap-policy-xacml-pdp-6f5db4cbb-fq4ss?timeout=15000 (anonymous) ...
[2021-08-19T19:52:57.276+00:00|INFO|HttpClient|DMAAP-source-POLICY-PDP-PAP] 	--> HTTP/1.1 200 OK

So, provided that there has not been changes in master since start of istanbul, 2 policy components using the same libraries and the same urls against dmaap on the same topic getting different responses, I am inclined to think that there is some problem with dmaap or the kubernetes service resolution, not sure to which underlying pod ip, xacml would connect to.   Also the connection refused message seem to indicate that 3905 may not be even open in the message-router IP that gives the error.

Has there been any changes lately in dmaap or something related to services that you think may cause it?

Looking some into the logs I see issues connecting to dmaap by drools-pdp, but in contrast other policy components have no issues for the same topic, all of the policy components use the same libraries so that's strange that some are successful getting to the message-router and others don't.   For example:
> 
> drools (rolling errors talking to dmaap):
> [2021-08-19T18:52:39.082+00:00|INFO|HttpClient|DMAAP-source-POLICY-PDP-PAP] GET https://message-router:3905/events/POLICY-PDP-PAP/3991b3eb-e81b-4f46-9b3e-2edd72c2aa91/onap-policy-drools-pdp-0?timeout=15000&limit=100 (anonymous) ...
> [2021-08-19T18:52:40.117+00:00|WARN|HttpClient|DMAAP-source-POLICY-PDP-PAP] Error executing HTTP request. Connect to message-router:3905 [message-router/10.0.233.91] failed: Connection refused (Connection refused); blacklisting for 2 minutes
> 
> xacml (all OK for same url and topic using the same libraries):
> [2021-08-19T19:52:42.316+00:00|INFO|HttpClient|DMAAP-source-POLICY-PDP-PAP] GET https://message-router:3905/events/POLICY-PDP-PAP/9d1ddf70-12be-41c9-a812-a3fb433faa1b/onap-policy-xacml-pdp-6f5db4cbb-fq4ss?timeout=15000 (anonymous) ...
> [2021-08-19T19:52:57.276+00:00|INFO|HttpClient|DMAAP-source-POLICY-PDP-PAP] 	--> HTTP/1.1 200 OK
> 
> So, provided that there has not been changes in master since start of istanbul, 2 policy components using the same libraries and the same urls against dmaap on the same topic getting different responses, I am inclined to think that there is some problem with dmaap or the kubernetes service resolution, not sure to which underlying pod ip, xacml would connect to.   Also the connection refused message seem to indicate that 3905 may not be even open in the message-router IP that gives the error.
> 
> Has there been any changes lately in dmaap or something related to services that you think may cause it?

BTW I see rolling error messages in message-router-kafka-0 -
https://logs.onap.org/onap-integration/gating//123386-2/k8s-teardown/k8s-teardown/kubernetes-status/pod-onap-message-router-kafka-0-message-router-kafka.log

which I don't see in message-router-kafka-1 and message-router-kafka-2, I wonder if those 3 replicas of that statefulset get synchronized and depending on where a client lands gets errors or not.   One experiment would be to reduce the number of replicas in message-router-kafka statefulset to 1 and see if that helps.
Patch Set 2: Code-Review+1

Patch Set 2: Code-Review-1

sth interesting happened to policy-mariadb the metrics container didn't came up properly:
https://logs.onap.org/onap-integration/gating//123386-2/k8s/k8s/kubernetes-status/pod-onap-policy-mariadb-0-policy-mariadb-metrics-logs.html

looks a little bit as a password issue but let's try to retrigger the gate to make sure that we don't break the master at this stage
